Class GameEngine

java.lang.Object
simcli.engine.GameEngine
public class GameEngine extends Object
Core coordinator of the simulation game.

GameEngine owns the main game loop, maintains references to all subsystems (time, world, rendering, input), and orchestrates the flow between player commands and simulation processing. It follows the Facade pattern — higher-level code only talks to the engine, while the engine delegates to specialised managers internally.

Key Collaborators

  • TimeManager — tick/day/time-of-day tracking
  • WorldManager — building layout and current location
  • InputHandler — translates raw input into commands
  • GameLoop — per-tick need decay, events, lifecycle
  • RandomEventManager — random event generation
  • LifecycleManager — aging, death, retirement

Dependency Injection

All core dependencies (IRenderer, SimulationLogger, TimeManager, etc.) are injected via constructors or the initCore(TimeManager, IRenderer) helper, ensuring the engine is fully testable with mock implementations.

    Description

    Constructs a GameEngine by restoring a previously saved world.

    Constructs a new GameEngine for a brand-new world.
    GameEngine(String worldName, List<Sim> startingNeighborhood, IRenderer renderer)
    Constructs a new GameEngine for a brand-new world with an explicit renderer (useful for testing or alternate UIs).

  • Constructor Details

    • GameEngine

      public GameEngine(String worldName, List<Sim> startingNeighborhood)
      Constructs a new GameEngine for a brand-new world.

      Initialises all subsystems, places every Sim inside the default residential building, and selects the first Sim as the active player.

      Parameters:
      worldName - the display name for this save slot.
      startingNeighborhood - the initial list of Sims (at least one).

    • GameEngine

      public GameEngine(String worldName, List<Sim> startingNeighborhood, IRenderer renderer)
      Constructs a new GameEngine for a brand-new world with an explicit renderer (useful for testing or alternate UIs).
      Parameters:
      worldName - the display name for this save slot.
      startingNeighborhood - the initial list of Sims (at least one).
      renderer - the renderer to use for all UI output.

    • GameEngine

      public GameEngine(String worldName, int currentTick, List<Sim> loadedNeighborhood, boolean isGameOver)
      Constructs a GameEngine by restoring a previously saved world.

      Rebuilds all subsystems from persisted state. The first non-dead Sim in loadedNeighborhood is selected as the active player.

      Parameters:
      worldName - the display name for this save slot.
      currentTick - the persisted tick counter to restore.
      loadedNeighborhood - the previously saved list of Sims.
      isGameOver - whether the game was already over when saved.

    • GameEngine

      public GameEngine(String worldName, int currentTick, List<Sim> loadedNeighborhood, boolean isGameOver, IRenderer renderer)
      Constructs a GameEngine by restoring a previously saved world with an explicit renderer.
      Parameters:
      worldName - the display name for this save slot.
      currentTick - the persisted tick counter to restore.
      loadedNeighborhood - the previously saved list of Sims.
      isGameOver - whether the game was already over when saved.
      renderer - the renderer to use for all UI output.

  • Method Details

    • getActivePlayer

      public Sim getActivePlayer()
      Returns the currently active (controlled) Sim.

    • setActivePlayer

      public void setActivePlayer(Sim sim)
      Switches the active player to the given Sim.
      Parameters:
      sim - the Sim to make active.

    • getWorldManager

      public IWorldManager getWorldManager()
      Returns the world/location manager used by this engine.

    • getNpcManager

      public NPCManager getNpcManager()
      Returns the NPC manager responsible for park NPCs.

    • getLifecycleManager

      public LifecycleManager getLifecycleManager()
      Returns the lifecycle manager handling aging and death.

    • run

      public void run(Scanner scanner)
      Runs the main game loop until the player saves-and-exits or all Sims die.

      Each iteration: renders the HUD, processes player input via InputHandler, advances the simulation clock via GameLoop, and periodically auto-saves.

      The method body is intentionally high-level; all detailed logic is delegated to private helper methods for readability and testability.

      Parameters:
      scanner - shared Scanner for reading player input.

    • getWorldName

      public String getWorldName()
      Returns the display name of this save slot.

    • getCurrentTick

      public int getCurrentTick()
      Returns the current simulation tick counter.

    • getNeighborhood

      public List<Sim> getNeighborhood()
      Returns the full list of Sims in this neighborhood.

    • isGameOver

      public boolean isGameOver()
      Returns true if the game-over condition has been reached.

    • getSessionTotalMoney

      public int getSessionTotalMoney()
      Returns the total money earned across all Sims this session.

    • getSessionTotalItems

      public int getSessionTotalItems()
      Returns the total items bought across all Sims this session.
